title: Which Slimming Treatments Work in Malta? An Honest Guide
excerpt: Grace had already booked fat freezing at another clinic. She'd seen an Instagram Reel, read the comments — mostly enthusiastic, a few vague — and put down a deposit the same evening.
body_text: Last reviewed by the Carisma Medical Team — 2026 Grace had already booked fat freezing at another clinic. She'd seen an Instagram Reel, read the comments — mostly enthusiastic, a few vague — and put down a deposit the same evening. She came to Carisma Slimming for a second opinion almost as an afterthought, mentioning it with the slight embarrassment of someone who isn't sure they've made the right decision. During the consultation, our team asked about her goals in detail. Grace had lost 12 kilograms over the previous year — a genuine achievement, one she was proud of. But her concern wasn't excess fat in the way she'd initially assumed. It was the loose skin across her abdomen, and a loss of muscle tone that made her feel softer rather than smaller. She was 52, going through menopause, and her body was behaving in ways her twenties-self would not have predicted. The honest answer wasn't fat freezing. Not yet. For Grace, EMS muscle stimulation and skin tightening were the better starting point — treatments that address the structural changes her body had undergone, not simply attempt to remove more fat from an area that had already lost significant volume. The right treatment is not always the loudest one. This guide exists because Grace's situation is not unusual. Women across Malta are asking which slimming treatments work in Malta — scrolling through clinic pages, making decisions based on Instagram, word of mouth, and whatever clinic offered the first available appointment. Some of those decisions are fine. Some cost money and time with minimal return. Knowing which treatments are suited to which concern — and why — changes the outcome before you ever walk through a clinic door. Key Takeaways - Not all slimming treatments target the same problem — matching treatment to concern is everything - Fat freezing works best on pinchable subcutaneous fat, not loose skin or muscle loss - EMS rebuilds muscle structure, particularly useful after weight loss or during menopause - Combining treatments in the right sequence compounds results better than any single modality - A medically supervised consultation — not social media — is the only reliable starting point How to Judge Whether a Slimming Treatment 'Works' What the evidence actually measures When we evaluate whether a body treatment is effective, we are looking at specific, measurable changes: reduction in subcutaneous fat layer thickness (measured by ultrasound or callipers), increase in muscle fibre thickness, improvement in skin laxity, or reduction in localised fat deposits — depending on the treatment. These are the metrics that appear in peer-reviewed literature. "It worked for my friend" is not evidence. Neither is a transformation photo with no information about lighting, angles, or how much time passed. In a medically supervised setting, we use standardised measurements before and after a treatment course so that results are objective, not impressionistic. That said, evidence from clinical studies reflects averages across populations. Results may vary for each individual — and that variation can be significant depending on factors like tissue density, hormonal status, starting point, and lifestyle during treatment. The difference between fat reduction and weight loss This distinction matters enormously. Body contouring treatments — fat freezing, lipocavitation, fat dissolving injections — reduce localised deposits of subcutaneous fat. They do not produce systemic weight loss in the way that a caloric deficit or a medically supervised weight loss programme does. You may lose centimetres without losing kilograms. You may find that a treated area is measurably slimmer while the scale barely moves. This is not a failure — it is a different mechanism working on a different problem. Understanding which problem you actually have is the starting point for choosing the right approach. Our slimming guide explains the difference between targeted fat reduction and whole-body weight loss in more detail. Fat Freezing: What the Evidence Shows Clinical studies on cryolipolysis Fat freezing — known clinically as cryolipolysis — delivers precisely controlled cooling to subcutaneous fat tissue, typically to around 4°C for 35 to 60 minutes per cycle. At this temperature, adipocytes (fat cells) are selectively damaged through a process of apoptosis — programmed cell death — while the overlying skin remains unharmed. The body then clears the treated cells gradually through the lymphatic system over the following weeks. This non-invasive approach to body contouring requires no downtime and no anaesthesia. The research is consistent and well-established. A systematic review by Ingargiola et al. (2015, Aesthetic Surgery Journal) analysed 19 studies and found a mean fat layer reduction of 14.67–28.5% in treated areas, depending on methodology. Results are typically visible at 4–8 weeks and maximal at approximately 3 months post-treatment. High patient satisfaction is reported across studies. In a medically supervised programme at Carisma Slimming, fat freezing is used for stubborn deposits that have proven resistant to diet and exercise — not as a substitute for lifestyle change, but as a complement to it. Who gets the best results and who does not Fat freezing works best on pinchable subcutaneous fat — the kind you can physically hold between your fingers. It is not effective on visceral fat (the deeper abdominal fat around organs), and it does not address muscle tone or skin laxity. Candidates who see the strongest results tend to be within a relatively healthy range of their goal weight, with localised areas of concern rather than significant overall excess. For someone like Grace — where loose skin and loss of muscle structure are the primary concern — fat freezing would have delivered limited benefit relative to her actual goal. Understanding this difference is exactly why a consultation precedes any treatment recommendation. Results may vary for each individual, which is why our approach is always assessment-first. You can read more about our fat freezing treatment at Carisma Slimming to understand the full process and what a treatment course involves. EMS Muscle Stimulation: Building Muscle While Reducing Fat The research behind electromagnetic muscle stimulation High-Intensity Focused Electromagnetic (HIFEM) technology delivers focused electromagnetic pulses that induce supramaximal muscle contractions — meaning contractions that exceed what voluntary physical effort can produce. A standard 30-minute session generates approximately 20,000 contractions in the targeted muscle group, far beyond anything achievable through conventional exercise in the same timeframe. These intense contractions stress the muscle fibres, triggering remodelling and hypertrophy (fibre thickening), while the metabolic demands of the process also contribute to localised fat breakdown in the treated area. A pivotal multicentre study by Kinney et al. (2019, Aesthetic Surgery Journal) using HIFEM on the abdomen found a mean 19.5% reduction in subcutaneous fat and a mean 16.3% increase in muscle thickness at 2-month follow-up. These are not whole-body results — they are localised to the treated area — but for women experiencing muscle loss during menopause or after significant weight loss, those numbers represent meaningful structural improvement. Realistic expectations from an EMS course EMS is not a replacement for physical training. It is a complement, particularly relevant for women who cannot exercise at the intensity required to produce meaningful muscle change — due to joint pain, fatigue, or the natural muscle atrophy that accompanies hormonal transition. For Grace, EMS was directly relevant. Menopause accelerates muscle loss; without intervention, the body composition shifts toward less muscle and more fat, even at stable weight. A medically supervised EMS course, combined with appropriate nutritional guidance, addresses this directly. You will not feel sore the way you would after a heavy gym session, but you will notice — within a few sessions — a change in how the treated area sits and feels. Carrying shopping becomes easier. Climbing stairs requires less thought. The non-scale changes often precede the visible ones. Explore our muscle stimulation programme to learn what a full treatment course at Carisma involves. Lipocavitation: Ultrasound Fat Reduction How effective is it? Lipocavitation uses low-frequency ultrasound waves to create micro-bubbles within subcutaneous fat tissue. These bubbles implode, disrupting the fat cell membrane and releasing the stored lipids into the interstitial space, where they are then processed and eliminated by the body's lymphatic system. It is a non-invasive body contouring method suited to larger surface areas. Clinical evidence for lipocavitation is less extensive than for cryolipolysis, but published studies show measurable reductions in circumference in treated areas, particularly when sessions are structured appropriately and the lymphatic system is supported between treatments. It is generally better suited to larger surface areas — thighs, abdomen, flanks — than smaller, more localised deposits. The mechanism depends entirely on the body's ability to clear released lipids efficiently. This is why what happens between sessions matters as much as the session itself: hydration, gentle movement, and avoiding alcohol in the 48 hours post-treatment all support the clearance process. Aftercare is not optional — it is part of the treatment protocol. Best combined with lymphatic drainage — why In a medically supervised approach, lipocavitation is typically paired with lymphatic drainage. This is not an upsell — it is a clinical decision rooted in mechanism. If the lymphatic system is congested or slow, the lipids released by cavitation are not cleared efficiently, reducing the measurable outcome. Lymphatic drainage stimulates the movement of lymph fluid, supporting the body's natural clearance pathway and compounding the effect of the cavitation session. At Carisma Slimming, our treatment planning accounts for this synergy from the outset, rather than treating each session in isolation. Explore our lipocavitation treatment and lymphatic drainage pages for full details on how these two modalities work together. Fat Dissolving Injections: Targeted and Long-Lasting Which areas respond best Fat dissolving injections use a synthetic form of deoxycholic acid — a substance naturally produced by the body to metabolise dietary fat — to permanently destroy fat cells in a precisely targeted area. Once destroyed, those cells do not regenerate; the result is permanent reduction of the treated deposit, provided body weight remains stable. This treatment is most effective on small, defined areas where the concern is a localised pocket of fat that resists all other approaches. The most common areas treated are the submental region (under the chin), the jowl area, and small localised deposits in the abdomen or flanks. It is not suitable for large volume fat reduction, and it requires multiple sessions for optimal outcome — typically two to four, spaced several weeks apart. Some temporary swelling and redness at the injection sites is expected and resolves within days. For women who have reached their goal weight but find one specific area consistently refusing to shift, fat dissolving injections can provide a precisely targeted solution that other treatments cannot replicate. Our fat dissolving treatment page covers the full process, session spacing, and what to expect during recovery. Skin Tightening: Supporting Results After Fat Loss When to add this to a treatment plan Skin laxity is one of the most under-discussed consequences of weight loss — and one of the most common reasons women feel dissatisfied despite significant achievement. When fat volume reduces rapidly, the skin that previously covered it may not contract at the same rate, particularly in women over 40 where skin elasticity has naturally diminished, and during menopause when collagen production slows further. Skin tightening treatments use radiofrequency or other energy modalities to stimulate collagen and elastin production in the deeper dermal layers, gradually firming and tightening the overlying skin. The process is gradual — the skin responds over weeks to months — but the outcome, for appropriate candidates, is meaningful improvement in tone and texture. This was central to Grace's treatment plan. She had worked hard to lose 12 kilograms. The skin tightening treatments were not about fixing something she had done wrong — they were about supporting the physical reality of her body's adaptation to that change. Adding skin tightening to a treatment course is most beneficial when: significant fat reduction has occurred in the previous 6–12 months, skin shows visible looseness or crepiness in the target area, and the client is at or approaching their target weight. Learn more about our skin tightening treatments and when they are recommended as part of a personalised plan. Choosing the Right Treatment for Your Goal in Malta Why a consultation beats searching alone Every treatment described in this guide works — within the population it is designed for, at the appropriate stage of a person's journey, applied to the right concern. The question is never simply "does fat freezing work?" The question is "does fat freezing address what I am actually dealing with, right now, given my body composition, goals, and medical history?" No article — however thorough — can answer that for you. What an article can do is give you the vocabulary and framework to have a much more informed conversation with a clinical team, so that you leave a consultation with a plan that is genuinely suited to you rather than the most popular treatment on social media that month. At Carisma Slimming, our consultations are assessment-led. We measure, we ask, we listen. We explain what each treatment does and does not do. We recommend based on what we observe — not on what is most available or most frequently requested. Combining treatments for compounding results Many of the most effective outcomes at our clinic come from treatment combinations where each modality addresses a different layer of the same concern. Fat freezing reduces the volume. EMS rebuilds the muscle structure underneath. Skin tightening improves the surface. Lymphatic drainage supports clearance throughout. None of this needs to happen at once, and in medically supervised programmes, the sequencing is as important as the selection. Starting in the right order, at the right intervals, with appropriate lifestyle support between sessions — that is where results compound rather than simply add. Book a free consultation at Carisma Slimming to discuss your goals and receive a personalised treatment plan from our clinical team in Malta. FAQs About Slimming Treatments in Malta Is fat freezing or EMS better for belly fat in Malta? It depends entirely on the type of belly concern. Fat freezing is more appropriate when there is a pinchable layer of excess subcutaneous fat — the kind that has persisted despite diet and exercise efforts. EMS is more appropriate when the primary concern is lack of muscle tone, softness after weight loss, or the gradual muscle atrophy that occurs during menopause. For many women, a combination of both — in the right sequence — produces better results than either alone. A medically supervised assessment is the only way to determine which applies to your specific situation. How many sessions of lipocavitation do I need to see results? Most clients begin to notice circumference changes after 3–4 sessions, with full results typically assessed after a course of 6–8 sessions. This is a guideline, not a guarantee — results may vary for each individual depending on tissue type, lifestyle between sessions, and how well the lymphatic clearance pathway is functioning. Pairing sessions with lymphatic drainage and maintaining adequate hydration between appointments supports the best outcomes. Our team will advise on the appropriate session frequency during your consultation. Are the results from body contouring treatments permanent? Fat cells destroyed by cryolipolysis or fat dissolving injections do not regenerate — in that sense, the reduction in those specific cells is permanent. However, remaining fat cells in the body can expand with weight gain, which is why body contouring is most effective as part of a broader approach that includes sustainable lifestyle support. EMS and skin tightening results are maintained with ongoing physical activity and collagen support. In medically supervised programmes, we address maintenance planning as part of the treatment conversation, not an afterthought. Can body treatments help loose skin after weight loss? Yes — skin tightening treatments are specifically designed to address this. Radiofrequency and other energy-based modalities stimulate collagen and elastin production in the deeper layers of the skin, gradually improving firmness and tone in areas where laxity has developed following weight loss. Results build over 8–12 weeks and are most significant for mild to moderate laxity. Severe laxity following very large weight loss may require additional medical assessment. This is one of the most commonly overlooked aspects of a body transformation journey, and our clinical team assesses skin quality as standard during consultations. Is it safe to combine multiple slimming treatments? Yes, when planned by an experienced clinical team in a medically supervised setting. The key is sequencing — some combinations work synergistically when performed in the right order and at appropriate intervals; others may need to be staged to allow the body's clearance systems to work effectively between sessions. At Carisma Slimming, we design combination plans based on your individual goals, treatment history, and how your body is responding. We do not stack treatments arbitrarily — every element of a plan has a clinical rationale. What slimming treatments show the fastest visible results? Fat dissolving injections and lipocavitation tend to produce the earliest visible circumference changes, often noticeable within 2–3 weeks of beginning treatment for appropriate candidates. Fat freezing results are slower to emerge — visible change typically appears at 4–8 weeks and peaks at 3 months — because the clearance process takes time. EMS and skin tightening changes are more gradual still, building over 6–12 weeks as structural and collagen responses develop. The fastest result and the best result are not always the same thing; our team will help you understand the realistic timeline for your chosen approach. You Deserve a Plan That Is Actually Built for You If you have spent time searching which slimming treatments work in Malta, scrolling through clinic pages, reading reviews that tell you nothing concrete — you are not confused because you are not trying hard enough. You are confused because the information available is rarely presented in context, and context is everything. Grace left her first consultation at Carisma with a three-step plan, a clear explanation of why each treatment had been recommended, and a timeline that reflected what her body — specifically her body, not an average — was likely to respond to. She did not leave with fat freezing. She left with something better: a plan that fit her actual situation. Picture yourself in eight weeks. Not a number on the scale — a moment. Pulling on a jacket and noticing how differently it sits across your shoulders. Walking out of a meeting without adjusting your posture to compensate for discomfort. Standing in front of the mirror and seeing structure where you had been seeing softness. That is what the right treatment, at the right time, with proper medically supervised guidance, can begin to build. Results may vary for each individual — but the starting point is always the same: an honest conversation with a team who will assess you properly. Book your free slimming consultation at Carisma today. Our team in Malta will take the time to understand your goals, assess your body honestly, and recommend the treatment path that gives you the best chance of results you can actually see. With you every step, Katya
